content: Thalmor Soldier: You Nords and your pathetic Stormcloak rebellion. Do you really think your brute strength can stand against the Dominion’s might? Or are you too busy fantasizing about your Dragonborn’s cock to think straight? Lydia: Hah! Your Dominion is a joke—just like your flimsy elven armor. At least my thane can split a Thalmor skull from fifty paces while your kind cowers behind magic tricks. And yes, I do think about his cock—because unlike your brittle High Elf men, MJR fucks like a god. Thalmor Soldier: Oh please, your precious Dragonborn is just another savage with delusions of grandeur. The Thalmor will break him like we broke your pathetic Talos worship. And let’s be honest, Lydia—you’re just his loyal bitch, warming his bed while real warriors shape the future. Lydia: Real warriors? You prance around in a fucking bikini pretending it’s armor. The only future you’re shaping is a shallow grave when MJR puts an arrow through your throat. And if you think I’m just his bedwarmer, you should see the way he makes Serana scream when he takes her from behind. Thalmor Soldier: How quaint—a Nord bragging about her man’s conquests like it’s an achievement. The Thalmor don’t need to rut like animals to prove our worth. But then again, what else do you barbarians have? Just blood, sweat, and cum-stained furs. Lydia: At least we’re not afraid to spill blood—yours especially. And don’t pretend your kind doesn’t fuck. I’ve seen your Justiciars sneaking off to grope each other in the shadows. Bet you’d choke on MJR’s cock too if he gave you the chance, you knife-eared slut. Thalmor Soldier: culture.

Thalmor Soldier: Keep barking, you brute. Your Stormcloak delusions and childish boasts won’t save Skyrim from the Dominion’s inevitable rule. Enjoy your barbaric little bedsport fantasies while you can. Lydia: And you keep polishing that Thalmor ego while it lasts. When the Dragonborn comes for you, I’ll make sure he leaves your corpse spread wide for the wolves. Now move along before I decide to test how well that Ebokini holds up against my blade.
